my_thoughts: The validation/redefinition works: vicious circle = fact (aporia), redefines: doing = struggle (observation itself = permanent core doing), gives instruction: endurance + specific actions. The student expressed despair (realizing paradoxical trap), which shows they understand: more efforts = more cannot 'do' (vicious circle). They'll likely accept the validation/redefinition. The episode is complete: definition → definition → distinction → refinement → despair → validation/redefinition. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Three-turn pattern: T1: Student asked for definition (seeking technical definition)—definition: super-effort = external intentionality/obedience. T2: Student requested distinction (seeking criterion for difference)—refinement: super-effort = conscious/purposive/non-required, ordinary = pretence. T3: Student expressed despair ('I feel I am getting into a vicious circle. The more efforts I make, the more I see I cannot 'do'')—realizing paradoxical trap via reports negative feedback loop and expresses despair. They understand: more efforts = more cannot 'do' (vicious circle). The ontological_shift mechanism validates: vicious circle = fact (aporia), redefines: doing = struggle (observation itself = permanent core doing), gives instruction: endurance + specific actions. Pattern complete: definition question → definition → distinction question → refinement → despair question → validation/redefinition. They've shifted: vicious circle = fact, doing = struggle (observation itself) (validation/redefinition).
visible_response:
Then make more efforts and realize it constantly;
make it more permanent.
You will
see that you are not always in the same state.
People in ordinary life cannot 'do', but for
you it is different—all the time you must try to do.
You are always identified and you
must not identify, you must watch your talk, you are constantly negative and you must
struggle against negative emotions, you must remember yourself.
All this is doing. You
must observe these things and try to change them.
